channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Israel says it is investigating whether Hamas' top leader Sinwar was killed in Gaza
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — The Israeli military said Thursday it was looking into whether Hamas’ top leader Yahya Sinwar was killed in a military operation in Gaza, while an Israeli airstrike on a school sheltering displaced Palestinians killed at least 15 people, including five children, acco
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA, SAMY MAGDY and JACK JEFFERY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Death of ex-One Direction member Liam Payne at 31 shocks fans around the world
item.description: BUENOS AIRES, Argentina (AP) — The death of Liam Payne, who shot to stardom as a member of British boy band One Direction and grappled with intense global fame while still in his teens, sent shockwaves across the world Thursday as Argentine investigators continued their investigation at the scene.
item.dc:creator: ISABEL DEBRE and KAITLYN HUAMANI, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: How did as many as 140 people die in one of Nigeria's deadliest tanker explosions?
item.description: Nigerian authorities have announced measures to correct lapses that resulted in the death of more than 140 people in an explosion as they were trying to scoop up fuel from an overturned fuel tanker on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Chinedu Asadu And Salim Umar, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: South Korean court acquits former police chief over deadly crowd crush
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — A South Korean court found the former police chief of the country’s capital and two other officers not guilty over a botched response to a Halloween crowd crush that killed nearly 160 people in 2022.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pakistani police fire tear gas at protesting students as anger spreads over alleged on-campus rape
item.description: LAHORE, Pakistan (AP) — Pakistani police fired tear gas and charged at student protesters who ransacked a college building Thursday, as anger spread over an alleged on-campus rape.
item.dc:creator: BABAR DOGAR, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Bangladesh court issues arrest warrant for ex-Prime Minister Sheikh Hasina over deaths of protesters
item.description: A special court in Bangladesh has issued arrest warrants for former Prime Minister Sheikh Hasina and 45 others, including her close aides, on charges of crimes against humanity during a student-led uprising in July and August that forced her to flee the country.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Vietnamese real estate tycoon sentenced to life for billions in fraud in government graft crackdown
item.description: A Vietnamese real estate tycoon has been convicted of fraudulently obtaining property worth billions of dollars and sentenced to life in prison in a case that has been a centerpiece of the government’s crackdown on corruption.
item.dc:creator: Hau Dinh And David Rising, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: North Korea says its revised constitution defines South Korea as 'hostile state' for first time
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — North Korea confirmed Thursday that its recently revised constitution defines South Korea as “a hostile state" for the first time, two days after it blew up front-line road and rail links that once connected the country with the South.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The EU thought it had a new migration plan. But many leaders say it may be too little, too late
item.description: European Union leaders are using a summit to seek ways to make the bloc a more hostile destination for migrants and asylum seekers.
item.dc:creator: Raf Casert, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: US long-range B-2 stealth bombers target underground bunkers of Yemen's Houthi rebels
item.description: U.S. long-range B-2 stealth bombers launched airstrikes targeting underground bunkers used by Yemen’s Houthi rebels.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Zelenskyy outlines his 'victory plan' to Ukraine's lawmakers, including a call to join NATO
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — President Volodymyr Zelenskyy told lawmakers Wednesday that Ukraine’s Western partners are increasing pressure to negotiate with Russia, but he hinted such talks would be unfavorable to Kyiv as he unveiled what he called his “victory plan” for the war.
item.dc:creator: SAMYA KULLAB and HANNA ARHIROVA, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: China's leader makes rare visit to province facing Taiwan following major war exercises
item.description: TAIPEI, Taiwan (AP) — China’s President Xi Jinping made a rare visit to a province facing Taiwan following military exercises that mobilized China's navy, air force, missile force and land troops to simulate a blockade of the self-governing island democracy that Beijing claims as its own territory a
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Taliban begins enforcing rule banning 'images of living beings' in Afghan media
item.description: The Taliban is preventing certain outlets from airing images of human beings or animals, according to officials. The rule pertains to certain provinces at the time being.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden to travel to Germany Thursday, delays African leg of trip
item.description: In Berlin, Biden will convene a meeting with German Chancellor Olaf Scholz to discuss continued support for Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Tanya Noury, NewsNation

item.title: US unveils sanctions targeting Hezbollah funding
item.description: The Biden administration announced new sanctions Wednesday against three individuals and four companies with alleged ties to revenue streams for the Lebanese militant group Hezbollah.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, The Hill

item.title: Navy jet crashes in Washington state; crew members missing
item.description: A search is ongoing for a Navy aircraft and two crew members who crashed east of Mount Rainier in Washington state Tuesday, according to the Navy. The EA-18G Growler, based out of Naval Air Station Whidbey Island, Wash., crashed just after 3:20 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, The Hill

item.title: Biden reschedules Angola trip for early December
item.description: President Biden intends to travel to Angola in the first week of December, the White House said Wednesday, rescheduling a trip that was scrapped as Hurricane Milton approached Florida last week.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: Zelensky unveils anticipated 'Victory Plan' for Ukraine war
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky on Wednesday publicly unveiled his “Victory Plan” for Ukraine’s defensive war against Russia, following closed-door pitches with world leaders and including the two U.S. presidential candidates.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Israel decides on possible Iran targets: 'Precise and deadly'
item.description: Israel reportedly has decided on which possible targets it could strike in Iran in response to an Oct. 1 barrage of missiles that Tehran fired at Israel.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pressure grows for countries to deliver on promised biodiversity targets at UN conference
item.description: Two years after reaching a historic biodiversity agreement, countries will gather next week to determine whether they are making progress on efforts to save Earth's plant and animal life.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY and CHRISTINA LARSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: UN says Yemen risks being dragged into Mideast conflict that could spiral out of control
item.description: UNITED NATIONS (AP) — Yemen risks being dragged further into the military escalation in the Middle East that keeps intensifying and could spiral out of control, the U.N. special envoy for the Arab world’s poorest nation said Tuesday. Hans Grundberg told the U.N.
item.dc:creator: EDITH M. LEDERER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: An Australian police sergeant likely to be charged over a Nazi salute
item.description: An Australian state police chief apologized to the Jewish community after a sergeant allegedly performed a Nazi salute.
item.dc:creator: Rod McGuirk, The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: US 'concerned' about reports of North Korean soldiers fighting alongside Russia in Ukraine
item.description: The U.S. says it is "concerned" about reports of North Korean soldiers fighting alongside Russian troops in the war against Ukraine, calling it "a new level of desperation.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hezbollah vows to expand attacks in Israel after deadly strike in Lebanon's Christian heartland
item.description: AITO, Lebanon (AP) — The day after a deadly Israeli airstrike in northern Lebanon – far from Hezbollah’s main area of influence – the militant group's acting leader said it would aim rockets into more areas of Israel.
item.dc:creator: KAREEM CHEHAYEB and BASSEM MROUE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Russian man rescued after 67 days adrift at sea. His family members didn't make it.
item.description: A Russian man was rescued in the Sea of Okhotsk after surviving for two months adrift in an inflatable boat without engine power, but his brother and nephew died.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Iran terror proxies amass on Israel’s borders in 'Ring of Fire’
item.description: Iran has developed the so called "Ring of Fire" around Israel to further expand its influence throughout the region. Experts note that violent terror groups sit across the Jewish state's borders.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: America's THAAD anti-missile system starts arriving in Israel as Pentagon says it will be 'fully operational'
item.description: The first components of a U.S. missile defense system aimed at protecting Israel from attacks by Iran have been delivered, according to the Pentagon.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Kenya's High Court rejects move to stop deputy president's impeachment debate
item.description: NAIROBI, Kenya (AP) — Kenya's High Court on Tuesday rejected an application by the deputy president's lawyers to stop the senate from debating an impeachment motion against him after parliament voted to remove him from office last week.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A Hong Kong zoo seeks answers after 9 monkeys die in 2 days
item.description: HONG KONG (AP) — Hong Kong's oldest zoo is seeking answers in a monkey medical mystery after nine animals died in two days, including three members of a critically endangered species.
item.dc:creator: KANIS LEUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Deadly Israel shooting ruled a terror attack
item.description: A police officer was killed and four others were injured when a terrorist opened fire near a highway in Ashdod, Israel, on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Father called UK police to confess to killing daughter, 10, in England after he fled to Pakistan
item.description: The father of a 10-year-old girl who was found dead in England called U.K. police, after flying to Pakistan, to say that he had killed his daughter, prosecutors said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: UN Security Council voices 'strong concern' for UN peacekeepers after Israeli attacks
item.description: It's the first statement by the powerful U.N. body since Israel's attacks on the positions of the peacekeeping force.
item.dc:creator: EDITH M. LEDERER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: UNIFIL ignored Hezbollah terror build up for 18 years, Israel's UN ambassador says
item.description: Amid fighting between Israel and Hezbollah terrorists, the U.N. is facing criticism that its peacekeeping force has failed to stop the Iran-terror proxy's military build up in southern Lebanon.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: China surrounds Taiwan in military exercise against independence
item.description: China has launched massive military exercises surrounding Taiwan in response to the island refusing to follow the rule of the Communist Party.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden-Harris admin accused of ‘too little, too late’ to save the people of war-torn, famine-stricken Sudan
item.description: Critics say the Biden administration is in danger of waiting too long to stop the killing and help the people of Sudan get humanitarian aid, as it makes a last-minute effort to make a difference.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Maybe advanced civilizations aren’t all that advanced: Scientist
item.description: Antonio Gelis-Filho writes that perhaps advanced civilizations have reached the limits of their technology, and we may be close to doing the same.
item.dc:creator: Rich Johnson, NewsNation

item.title: Yemen’s Houthi rebels attack a Liberian-flagged ship in the Red Sea
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — Yemen's Houthi rebels attacked a Liberian-flagged chemical tanker on Thursday in the Red Sea, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, NewsNation
